theScene: Oh? Worried I might have some other lovers still floating around? I suppose I can't blame you, It's not like I'm a blushing virgin anymore... Haven't been for a long time.

Kotone goes deathly quiet as her smile freezes and slowly slides into a neutral expression, but her eyes smolder with hatred as she gazes into your eyes.
Are you sure you want to know [ThePlayerName]? Really and truly?
You give her a brief nod as she continues to motionlessly gaze at you, making you shiver involuntarily as she almost seems to stare into your very soul.
Kotone goes deathly quiet as her smile freezes and slowly slides into a neutral expression, but her eyes smolder with hatred as she gazes into your eyes.
Are you sure you want me to tell you that again, [ThePlayerName]? Really and truly?
You give her a brief nod as she continues to motionlessly gaze at you, making you shiver involuntarily as she almost seems to stare into your very soul.
She continues to unflinchingly lock her gaze onto yours as she flashes you a small, bitter grin.

You know, when I was a little girl, my mother always used to tell me stories every night about how she met my father up until the point that he died in a pointless war somewhere, making sure to let me know how it was love at first sight and they'd loved each other for every waking moment since. Naturally, I wanted the exact same thing they did.
After all, what girl doesn't? I spent years of my life hoping for the day when I would meet the man of my dreams, the soulmate who I would live with for as long as I possibly could.
She lets out a contemptuous scoff as her eyes shine with frozen rancor despite her entire body being otherwise utterly motionless.
...And when I was young, naïve, and stupid, I fell for the first handsome man I ever met.
My mother warned me about him as soon as she met him, but I didn't care. He loved me back, and he made me feel alive in what felt like the first time in my entire life.
Kotone's voice seems utterly detached as she speaks to you, but you can hear the faintest hint of barely restrained rage pressing through her steely control as her smile twists in contempt.

In hindsight, it was obvious to see that he was as scummy and manipulative as they come, but I was blinded by my love and didn't notice a single thing other than his dazzling smile and beautiful face.
A shiver runs through her otherwise motionless body as her voice becomes chillingly calm, and her eyes suddenly blaze with furious, uncontrollable hatred.
And I continued to think that he was the perfect kind of gentleman for a long time... Until the day we finally had sex.

He'd been trying to convince me to do it with him for a week at that point, and I was over the moon about the fact that I was going to have my first time with my beloved, and he was waiting for me when I snuck out of my house that night, telling me how happy he was as he led me to a secluded shack by the edge of town.
A harsh bark of laughter escapes her as she unceasingly stares at you while continuing to talk in her calm, measured voice.

But as soon as I entered that dim little building with him, he locked the door, pushed me down onto the straw mat, and fucked me roughly and painfully with no regards to my feelings, only stopping once he was sated. I laid on the ground, shivering and in agony from how I was treated by him, and the one last bit of romance my naïve heart had left dimly flickered when I suddenly grew my second tail.

Kotone's glacially piercing eyes seem to focus even more intensely than ever before onto your own, and you can barely even remember to draw in a breath at the sheer amount of animosity being shown to you as her hands suddenly clench into tight fists.
Then, after a few scant moments of getting to enjoy what seemed to be the only good thing about losing my virginity, he ripped that tail off.
She goes completely silent as she lets her words sink in, her quiet stare filling you with a creeping feeling of fear and revulsion.

I was in so much pain it's hard to describe, but once it was over... It felt even worse. This was more than being just maimed. A kitsune's tails are directly linked to their soul... I was left feeling lesser... Hollow...
She trails off and blinks, drawing a short breath, her icy gaze piercing through you as she continues.
Have you ever had a part of your very soul ripped away, [ThePlayerName]? Something that can never really be replaced no matter how much time passes? Because I did that day.
There's a brief pause before Kotone lets out a bitter laugh as her face twists into a sneer, and she stares into your eyes with pure loathing.
So, as I laid there on the ground, in so much agony that I couldn't even scream, he told me everything.

He had no interest in me as a person, and he never did. He was part of a criminal organization that specialized in the obtaining and selling of rare monster girl components, and he laughed as he told me how much money he was going to make off of my tail, saying that he was going to keep me in that little shack forever so I'd always be his personal source of income.

He abandoned me there, locking the door behind him as he carried off my tail, and when I finally managed to find my own voice through the pain, I screamed and cried for someone, anyone to help for hours and hours until my voice went hoarse, then screamed some more. Nobody came, [ThePlayerName].
Kotone's revulsion and vitriolic resentment wash over you in an almost physical wave of raw emotion. She continues to speak in her calm, placid voice, but her body lightly shakes with contempt.
I cried as I huddled in the center of that cold room, [ThePlayerName]. I cried and cried until I fell asleep... I still have nightmares of that night sometimes.
There's a moment of silence as her face suddenly goes neutral again, but her eyes continue to glimmer darkly as she begins to talk once more.
...I woke up the next morning to the sound of a lock shattering before the door bursting open, and my mother came in with a concerned look that transformed into shock, then cold, uncontrollable rage.

I've never felt so much magical energy in my entire life as she carried me out of the shack on her back, and within moments, she sent out a blazing tendril of magic shooting out towards the village. It moved so quickly that it cracked the air like thunder as it moved through the air.
Less than half a minute later, it brought back the man who had his way with me in its grasp, filling the air with his pitiful screams as that flaming tendril seared the flesh it touched.
Kotone's face is utterly devoid of expression as she recounts the story, making you shiver at the sheer apathy of her voice combined with the hatred in her eyes.

My mother didn't hesitate for a moment as she silently threw him into that tiny little shack, slammed the door shut with her magic, and set fire to the whole thing, burning so hot that the ground underneath the building began to melt as he screamed for mercy, only to slowly stop as he died.
By the time my mother was done, the only thing that remained was a blackened smear on the ground, and she carried me back home in her arms without saying a single word the whole time.
Kotone shivers as she suddenly brings her arms up to hug herself, and she finally averts her eyes from you as they flash with an unbearable sadness.

...My mother tried everything she could to help me cope with what happened, but nothing she seemed to do worked. I wasn't the cheerful, lovestruck girl I used to be anymore, I was someone... {i}else{/i}...
I despised all humans, I kept lashing out and doing horrible things to those who loved me, and...
She trails off as her face contorts with guilt, suddenly seeming to become much smaller as she stands before you.
...And I left home, abandoning my mother as I went.
Kotone goes silent for a long moment before she lets out a sigh and suddenly looks up at you, seeming more vulnerable than you've ever seen her before.

I spent the next seven years wandering with a... companion I'd made on my travels, tracking down and killing any and all members of the organization that {i}he{/i} was from. I was intent on getting revenge for what he'd done to me.
Guilt and self-loathing slowly seep into her voice as she continues, her fists clenched so tight that her nails sink into her palms, threatening to draw blood.

But I wasn't content with just targeting that organization, [ThePlayerName]. I-I didn't stop at just those criminals, I went out of my way to {i}mentally and physically{/i} torture anyone I found out they were close to. Family, friends... i-it didn't matter to me at the time... I only spared children...
She can no longer keep eye contact with you. Shame, guilt, and self-hatred contort her expression and voice.

I would like to say it was just to get information on the organization, and where its last few leaders were hiding from me... But I know I just wanted to put them all through the same hell I had been through.
Despite trying to keep herself calm, she raggedly breathes in and out, swallowing with difficulty.

It... it was selfish. Cruel. Gratuitous. It wasn't anything anyone should be proud of or allowed to do. But at the time, it seemed right. It {i}felt{/i} right... But most of all, it felt... liberating. Intoxicating, even.
She shakes her head in disgust, then braces herself for the next part of her story, carrying on through the painful memory.
And after seven years of ruthless vendetta, once they were {i}finally{/i} wiped off the face of the world, I returned home.
Her expression twists into one of profound grief as her gaze turns downcast. After a moment of silence, she bites her lip, and carries on in a choked voice.

...But instead of coming back to a house where I could gloat about how I'd finally gotten revenge, I came back to an empty home. I knew something was wrong, and I think a part of me knew what had happened, but I refused to think about it as I looked around. I went to check my family's graveyard, expecting my mother to be praying at my father's grave. Instead, I found a new headstone sitting right next to it. My... my mother's.

Kotone goes quiet once more as she tries to get herself under control. Not knowing how to react, sensing all the tension and emotion she's under, you don't dare say or do anything. When she speaks again, she lets out a bitter laugh, her eyes gleaming with anguish.

Everything felt like it was falling apart around me as I scoured the house in disbelief. It all felt like some kind of sordid joke, I just couldn't believe it... Not until I found a note she'd left on my old bed. You know what it said, [ThePlayerName]?
Her voice cracks slightly as she looks back up at you, her face overwhelmed with a mixture of painful emotions. She intones by heart, each syllable holding back a wracking sob:
{i}'My dearest Kotone, I'm sorry I wasn't strong enough to protect you. I pray you find peace.'{/i}
Her gaze falls as she quivers with cold self-loathing and guilt, her face straining as she grits her teeth to hold back the tears building in the corners of her eyes.

I felt like the world was falling out from under me. I had been such a {i}stupid, petulant child{/i}. All those years, I'd fancied myself some great dispenser of justice, but all I did was sow misery and destruction.
She takes a moment to steady herself, closing her eyes and focusing her breathing for a moment before continuing.

In a daze, I left the house. Asking around town, I soon found out that my mother had died of grief less than a year after I had left. She was so distraught, thinking that she'd lost her only child forever and that she'd never see me again. I had been too caught up in my own foolish quest for vengeance to {i}even know{/i} she had passed on. I could have come back earlier, I could have sought news, I could have sent her a letter... I...

She shivers and tightly hugs herself as a single tear runs down one of her cheeks. Absent-mindedly wiping it with her sleeve, she stares down at the ground, an expression of self-damning regret painted on her face.

As I wandered around my old home, I saw how lifeless it was now that my mother was gone. I thought back to all the things she used to talk to me about, all the lessons and stories... And it finally struck me what I'd been doing with my own life. The memories of all those families and lives I had ended or ruined without a second thought made me realize how I’d been blind, self-serving, and drunk on my own {i}'self-righteous'{/i} revenge... How I’d become so much like {i}him{/i}.

She practically spits the words out in disgust before letting out a long, shuddering sigh, bowing her head for a moment to glare downwards. When she returns her gaze back up to you, her face shows a small joyless smile, tinged with grim, unfathomable sadness.

...There's so much I've regretted in my life, [ThePlayerName]. Too many things to count. But not being there for my mother... running off on a quest of revenge... blindly destroying peoples' lives... I'll never be able to forgive myself for any of it... And neither should you. Neither should anyone. There are burdens you have to carry to your grave.

I spent days in listless despair. I... honestly considered just ending it all right there. But as I spent time in my old home, I remembered how I had grown up. How much love my father and mother had surrounded me with. I knew that whatever could happen, they’d only want the best for me.

So from that day onwards, I resolved to try and become a person my mother would have wanted me to be. Gradually, I got better with the... hatred, but some wounds just don't heal, no matter how much time passes.
A long silence fills the room as Kotone averts her eyes to stare at one of the walls, before she quietly whispers out a bit more...
A long silence fills the room as Kotone averts her eyes to stare at one of the walls...
I... I wouldn't blame you if this is where you want to call our relationship off, [ThePlayerName]. With all the things I've done, I... would understand.

The last few Demon Queens have been putting in quite the effort to try and smooth things out with humans and monsters... To varying degrees of success.

Unless you were actively trying to hurt someone. Then it'd just pass through. Well, normally, whatever the enchantment is, it isn't 100% foolproof that's for sure. Accidents can still happen on occasion, but direct violence is almost a thing of the past.
